name: New-York Historical Society (DiMenna Children's History Museum)
address: 170 Central Park West, New York, NY 10024

hours.notes: Pay-as-you-wish Fridays 5-8 PM. DiMenna Children's History Museum on the lower level.

pricing.details: Adults $24, Seniors $19, Students $13, Children 5-13 $6, Under 5 free. Pay-as-you-wish Fri 5-8 PM.

description: Steps from 79th & Broadway, this museum includes the DiMenna Children's History Museum on its lower level with interactive exhibits about NYC and American history. The children's museum is designed for ages 8-13, but toddlers enjoy the sensory exploration, dioramas, and play-based gallery games. Strollers permitted throughout.

name: Whitney Museum of American Art

hours.notes: Free Fri 5-10 PM. Free 2nd Sunday of every month. Under 25 always free.

pricing.details: Adults $30, Under 25 always free. Free Fri 5-10 PM and 2nd Sundays. Floor 1 gallery always free.

description: American art museum in the Meatpacking District with a joint program with CMOM offering hands-on artmaking for ages 0-6 on Saturday mornings (11 AM-3 PM) including sand play, lightboards, building projects, movement activities, bubbles, and music circles. Outdoor terraces with Hudson River views are great for toddler breaks. Strollers permitted throughout.

name: El Museo del Barrio

hours.notes: Open Thu-Sun only. Located on Museum Mile at 104th St.

pricing.details: Pay what you wish. Suggested: $9 adults, $5 students/seniors, children free.

description: Latin American and Caribbean art museum on Museum Mile specializing in Puerto Rican and Latinx culture. Offers bilingual family workshops, hands-on craft activities, live music and dance performances, and seasonal carnivals. The colorful galleries and interactive programming make it engaging for toddlers, and the pay-what-you-wish policy makes it very accessible.

name: Asia Society and Museum

hours.notes: Free admission on Fridays.

pricing.details: Adults $15, Students/Seniors $10, Under 16 free. Free on Fridays. Family Day events: $20 non-members, $15 members, children over 2 $10.

description: Cultural center at 70th & Park hosting Family Day events with lion dances, martial arts demos, shadow puppet-making, music, crafts, and storytelling celebrating Asian cultures. Regular family programs include hands-on art workshops and interactive cultural activities. The intimate scale and sensory-rich Family Day events work well for toddlers.

name: Japan Society

hours.notes: Free First Fridays 5-7 PM. Gallery hours listed; event hours vary.

pricing.details: Gallery: $15 non-members, $10 students/seniors. Family membership $125 (2 adults + 4 children). Free First Fridays 5-7 PM.

description: Cultural center near the UN offering Kids & Family programs with storytelling, art workshops, and traditional Japanese festival celebrations including New Year, Children's Day, and the 7-5-3 Ceremony with performances, crafts, and hands-on activities. The Kamishibai (picture story) tradition is particularly engaging for toddlers.

name: Museum of Arts and Design (MAD)

hours.notes: Open 7 days. Free docent tours Wed 11:30 AM, Thu 2:30 PM, Fri-Sun 11:30 AM & 2:30 PM.

pricing.details: Adults $18, Seniors $14, Students $12, Under 18 free. Under 12 free.

description: Museum at Columbus Circle showcasing craft, art, and design with hands-on family workshops on select Sundays. The tactile nature of craft and design exhibits (textiles, ceramics, jewelry, furniture) appeals to toddlers' sensory curiosity. Free for children under 18. The 9th-floor restaurant has Central Park views for post-visit snacks.

name: New Museum

hours.notes: Pay-what-you-wish Thu 7-9 PM. Free for under 18. New OMA-designed expansion opened March 2026.

pricing.details: Adults $25, Seniors $22, Students $19, Under 18 free. Pay-what-you-wish Thu 7-9 PM.

description: Contemporary art museum on the Lower East Side with a major new OMA-designed expansion that opened in March 2026. Hosts free Family Day events with art-making activities and gallery exploration. Immersive contemporary installations can captivate toddlers with light, color, and sound. Free for all children under 18.

name: New Victory Theater

hours.notes: Box office open daily. Show schedule varies by production. Lap seats for under-3s available by calling 646-223-3010.

pricing.details: Tickets $25-$72 depending on seating. Members save 30%. Lap seats for under 3 available (call 646-223-3010).

description: New York's premier theater for kids and families on 42nd Street, offering a full season of world-class performances including circus, puppetry, dance, and music. The 2025-26 season includes shows for ages 3+ like Chicka Chicka Boom Boom: The Musical. Free interactive activities in the lobby before and after every show. Lap seats available for children under 3.

name: Vital Theatre Company (Pinkalicious & more)

hours.notes: Shows at 11:00 AM on select days. Check schedule for specific dates. Performs at Actors Temple Theatre.

pricing.details: Front Mezzanine $59.50, Premium Orchestra $79.50, VIP Orchestra $119 (includes backstage tour + wand). Shows ~60 min, no intermission.

description: Children's theater company performing at the Actors Temple Theatre in Midtown, staging shows designed for ages 2-8 including the long-running Pinkalicious The Musical and The Wizard of Oz. The 50-60 minute runtime with no intermission is perfect for toddlers' attention spans. Colorful, musical productions with audience interaction.

name: TADA! Youth Theater

hours.notes: Show schedule varies by production. Check website for dates/times. Near 28th St R/W station.

pricing.details: Affordable ticket prices, among the lowest in NYC. Contact box office: 212-252-1619 x5 or Boxoffice@tadatheater.com.

description: Award-winning youth theater between Broadway and 5th Ave producing original musicals recommended for ages 3+, performed entirely by young people ages 8-18. Shows feature music, puppetry, and physical comedy. Known for some of the most affordable children's theater tickets in the city. The intimate venue and high-energy performances engage toddlers.

name: Central Park Zoo & Tisch Children's Zoo
address: East 64th Street & 5th Avenue, New York, NY 10021

hours.notes: Hours listed are winter; summer hours extend to 5:00 PM weekdays, 5:30 PM weekends. Tisch Children's Zoo is seasonal (Apr-Oct).

pricing.details: Adults $22.95 (full) or $15.95 (limited, no 4D), Children 3-12 $16.95/$9.95, Seniors $18.95/$12.95, Under 2 free. Includes Tisch Children's Zoo.

description: Compact, toddler-perfect zoo in Central Park with the Tisch Children's Zoo where kids can pet and feed goats, sheep, cows, pigs, and rabbits. Offers Caregiver & Me programs for ages 2-3. The small scale means no stroller marathon. Sea lions, penguins, snow monkeys, and a tropical rainforest zone. 4D theater included with full admission.

name: Prospect Park Zoo

hours.notes: Hours may vary seasonally. Summer hours extend later on weekends.

pricing.details: Adults $8, Seniors $6, Children 3-12 $5, Under 2 free.

description: Small, intimate Brooklyn zoo ideal for toddlers with a Discovery Trail, animal encounters, and farm-themed exhibits. Offers Toddler Time summer programs for ages 2-3. Much less overwhelming than the Bronx Zoo, with prairie dogs, sea lions, dingoes, and a barnyard. The compact layout is perfect for short toddler attention spans.

name: New York Aquarium

hours.notes: Last entry 1 hour before closing. Free Wednesdays after 3 PM (advance tickets required). All tickets require advance reservation.

pricing.details: Off-peak: Adults $29.95, Seniors $27.95, Children 3-12 $25.95. Peak: Adults $32.95, Seniors $29.95, Children 3-12 $27.95. Under 2 free. Free Wed after 3 PM.

description: The only aquarium in NYC, located on the Coney Island boardwalk. Toddlers love the sea lion shows, touch tanks, shark exhibits, and the Ocean Wonders pavilion. The stroller-accessible layout and 4D theater make it a full toddler outing. Combine with a boardwalk stroll along Coney Island beach afterward.

name: Brooklyn Botanic Garden

hours.notes: Bloom season hours (through May 10). Discovery Garden open seasonally. Early Learner drop-ins Wed & Fri 10:30 AM-12:30 PM.

pricing.details: Adults $22 ($23.67 online), Students/Seniors $16, Under 12 free.

description: 52-acre garden with the Discovery Garden, a one-acre landscape for kids with pond, woodland, meadow habitats, and hands-on nature exploration. Drop-in Early Learner programs (ages 0-4) on Wednesdays and Fridays include potting plants, crafts, story time, and sensory activities. Toddler gardening classes for ages 2-3 run spring through early summer. Under 12 always free.

name: New York Botanical Garden (Everett Children's Adventure Garden)

hours.notes: Children's Adventure Garden guided activities: weekdays 1:30-5 PM, weekends 11 AM-5 PM. Self-guided exploration 10 AM-5:30 PM.

pricing.details: Weekday: Adults $35, Seniors/Students $31, Children 2-12 $15. Weekend: Adults $39, Seniors/Students $35, Children 2-12 $17. Under 2 free.

description: 250-acre garden with the 12-acre Everett Children's Adventure Garden featuring tunnels, boardwalks, a wetland pond, sensory-rich planting areas, and the Seedling Circle with plant-inspired instruments. Nature Explorers programs for ages 2-3 introduce toddlers to the natural world. Drop-in family programs with hands-on science activities run daily. The outdoor scale gives toddlers room to roam.

name: CAMP (Bluey x CAMP Experience)

hours.notes: Open 7 days. Advance booking recommended for immersive experience as sessions sell out. Front retail store is free to browse.

pricing.details: Immersive experience ~$38/person for ages 2+ (varies by day/time, weekends higher). Under 2 free. Front store free to browse. Also offers Slime Bar and Craft Studio.

description: Family experience store at 16th & 5th Ave with a ticketed immersive Bluey adventure where families step inside Bluey's house for counselor-led activities and a meet-and-greet with Bluey and Bingo (~1 hour). Also features The Slime Bar and a Craft Studio. The front store is free to explore with toys and activities. Perfectly designed for toddlers who love Bluey.

name: Sloomoo Institute

hours.notes: Hours vary; check sloomoo.com for current schedule. Advance reservation recommended.

pricing.details: Starting at ~$39-50/person. Each ticket includes DIY slime bar (custom 8oz slime to take home). Under 5 must be closely supervised.

description: Immersive slime experience in SoHo with ASMR tunnels, slime waterfalls, interactive slime stations, and a DIY Bar where you design a custom slime to take home. Stroller and wheelchair accessible with sensory-friendly accommodations. Toddlers love the tactile, messy sensory play. All ages welcome; under 5 must be closely supervised by parent/guardian.

name: INTER

hours.notes: Open 7 days (Tue opens at 1 PM). Strollers must be folded and stored in lobby. Book online in advance.

pricing.details: Starting at ~$35/person (varies by time slot). Under 4 free. Plan for 2-3 hours.

description: Intergalactic immersive adventure in a former bank building at Broadway & Canal. Families board a starship and explore 10 zones with motion-sensitive displays that respond to movement, lights, and sound. Gentle pace and open areas are toddler-friendly; 2-5 year olds thrive on the sensory play as colors shift around them. Under 4 free. Strollers must be stored in lobby.

name: Arte Museum New York

hours.notes: Last admission: weekdays 5:30 PM, weekends 7:30 PM. Some rooms do not allow strollers due to mirrored glass floors.

pricing.details: Weekday: Adults $55, Children 4-12 $45. Weekend: Adults $65, Children 4-12 $55. Ages 0-3 free. Visit takes 60-90 min.

description: Immersive media art exhibition at Chelsea Piers with floor-to-ceiling projections of nature scenes including waterfalls, forests, and auroras across themed rooms with mirrors creating infinite-seeming spaces. Children ages 0-3 enter free. Caveat for toddlers: some rooms are dark with mirrored floors, requiring close supervision, and some rooms restrict strollers. Best for toddlers who enjoy visual spectacles and aren't spooked by dark environments.

name: Long Island Children's Museum

hours.notes: Open on most public school holidays 10 AM-5 PM. Tot Spot designed for birth to age 3.

pricing.details: $14 per person ages 3+. Under 3 free. Members free.

description: Dedicated children's museum in Garden City with the Tot Spot, a sensory-rich area designed specifically for birth to age 3 with soft play structures, age-appropriate manipulatives, mirrors, and gentle sounds. Daily early childhood programs supplement the interactive exhibits. A top destination for toddlers on Long Island, reachable via LIRR from Penn Station to Garden City.
